'''Xim's empire''' was the massive [[Empire (disambiguation)|empire]] created by [[Xim/Legends|Xim the Despot's]] conquests, and which [[Third Battle of Vontor|fractured]] on his death.

==History==
The empire had its origins in the [[Kingdom of Cron]], a fiefdom carved out during the [[Cronese Sweeps]] by [[Xer VIII]], the [[Pirate/Legends|pirate]] who became Xim's father. Based on [[Argai]] and containing at least thirteen other planets, it was the first territory ruled by Xim when he came into power in [[25,130 BBY]].

The first ten years of Xim's reign were known as his [[Xim's Expansionist Period|Expansionist Period]]. During this time he [[Battle of Jhantoria|conquered]] much of the [[Tion Cluster/Legends|Tion Cluster]], including the [[Livien League]] in [[25,130 BBY]] which was established within it. At its height, Xim's empire included thousands of worlds and stretched from the [[Radama Void/Legends|Radama Void]] to [[Maw Cluster/Legends|the Maw]]. Xim established a secret police, the [[GenoHaradan]], and used terror tactics including mass executions to maintain order. He also pioneered the construction of [[war-robot]]s to bolster his combat forces. Xim used orbital fortresses during his campaigns. At the height of the empire's power, Xim held palaces on Argai and [[Raxus Prime/Legends|Raxus]], great vaults on [[Dellalt/Legends|Dellalt]], and a monolithic statue on [[Desevro/Legends|Desevro]].

However, Xim's swath of conquest would soon bring him into conflict with the [[Hutt Empire]], the preeminent power in [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]] and already a historic enemy of his Tionese subjects. For some time, they had [[Xim Wars|quarreled]] over control of the [[Si'Klaata Cluster/Legends|Si'Klaata Cluster]]. It was during this period that the orbital fortress now known as [[Terman Station]] was placed near [[Sriluur/Legends|Sriluur]] in the [[Periphery/Legends|Periphery]]. In [[25,102 BBY]] Xim [[Sack of Ko Vari|decimated]] the [[Hutt/Legends|Hutt]] colony world of [[Boonta/Legends|Ko Vari]]. However, shortly after, the Hutts under [[Kossak Inijic Ar'durv|Kossak]] in turn destroyed an entire fleet of Xim's at the [[First Battle of Vontor|First Battle of]] [[Vontor]].

Shortly later, a [[Second Battle of Vontor|second battle]] was fought at Vontor, with an inconclusive result. This led to the Hutts imposing the [[Treaty of Vontor]] on the [[Vodran (species)/Legends|Vodrans]], [[Nikto/Legends|Niktos]], [[Klatooinian/Legends|Klatooinians]], and [[Weequay/Legends|Weequay]], gaining those species' services as [[Mercenary/Legends|mercenaries]]. In the [[Third Battle of Vontor|third battle]] for Vontor in [[25,100 BBY]], Xim's forces were totally decimated, with his treasure ship, the [[Queen of Ranroon/Legends|''Queen of Ranroon'']], being one of the few forces of his to survive. Xim was captured and taken to [[Nal Hutta/Legends|Nal Hutta]], where he died a prisoner in Kossak's palace.

Following the death of Xim, his empire quickly collapsed under the Hutt invasion. The Kingdom of Cron reasserted its independence, and new states such as the [[Jaminere Marches]] and the [[Honorable Union of Desevro and Tion|Honorable Union of Desevro & Tion]] were quickly formed out of the former territory of the empire. During the [[Tionese War]] ([[24,000 BBY]]), the Tionese crumbled under the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]].

The GenoHaradan would continue to exist for at least the next 20,000 years, evolving into a criminal syndicate. Xim would continue to be a hero of the Tionese people for millennia, despite his brutish ways.

==Economy and society==
Occupying thousands of worlds, Xim's empire became extremely wealthy, with an economy generally geared towards war fighting: [[Shipyard/Legends|shipyards]] and factories on [[Jaminere/Legends|Jaminere]], [[Cadinth]] and [[Thanium]] provided the equipment and materiel for Xim's war machine. Other worlds built merchant ships and beacons, and Tionese artisans sought to outdo each other: Xim expanded or built vast palaces on [[Raxus Prime/Legends|Raxus Prime]], [[Amarin]] and [[Argai]], and a royal retreat at [[Nuswatta]].

The famous [[Forbidden Gardens of Nuswatta/Legends|Forbidden Gardens of Nuswatta]] perhaps exemplified the nature of Xim's rule: for all but Xim's court, gazing upon the Gardens brought the death sentence, and according to legend they were tended by a cadre of slaves blinded at birth. Other rumors told of failed military officers, traitors, or conquered enemies being executed there. Xim also controlled his conquests through his dreaded secret police, the [[GenoHaradan]], and the use of terror tactics such as mass executions.

==Military==
Xim the Despot built a powerful military-industrial complex from lost [[Rakata/Legends|Rakatan]] technology. The scale of his armies and conquests meant that numerous examples of Xim's weaponry survived to the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]] era. Xim's [[Infantry/Legends|infantry]] were divided into several castes. Most were [[Janissary/Legends|janissaries]] taken from occupied worlds, trained from childhood to serve as soldiers. They wore [[Armor/Legends|armor]] and helmets of lightweight ceramic alloy and carried either [[Slugthrower/Legends|slugthrowers]] or [[heatbeam]]s, which used [[Laser/Legends|lasers]] to generate jets of [[Plasma/Legends|plasma]]. Heatbeamers wore tough, fireproof padded gloves and [[Kama/Legends|''kamas'']] a protection against their weapons' superheated exhausts. The ''kama'' would later be adopted by the [[Thyrsus/Legends|Thyrsus]] [[Sun Guard]] and by the [[Mandalorian/Legends|Mandalorian]] people as part of their culture.

Among Xim's elite units were the [[Duinarbulon Star Lancers]], who wore gloss-black [[Kiirium/Legends|kiirium]] ceramic armor and carried advanced (for the time) [[Beam tube/Legends|beam tubes]], distant ancestors of the [[Blaster/Legends|blaster]]. The infantry was supported by tracked [[Tank/Legends|tanks]], armored [[Groundcar/Legends|groundcars]], and [[floater]]s armed with beam tube cannons. Elite vehicles were armored with [[Kiirium/Legends|kiirium]] to reflect energy weapons. Xim's most famous soldiers were the war-robots, three-meter tall [[Battle droid/Legends|battle droids]] armed with heatbeams, particle dischargers, and [[pulse-wave cannon]]s. The War-Robots were typically kept in reserve for use as shock troops.

Xim's [[Warship/Legends|warships]] were smaller than [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]]-era craft and were all unique designs, since the ancient Tionese saw warship construction as an art form as opposed to an industry. Under the [[Anaxes War College System/Legends|Anaxes War College System]], the bulk of Xim's warships would be considered [[Frigate/Legends|frigates]] or [[Corvette/Legends|corvettes]], and included [[Argaian hemiolia]], [[Livien cutter]]s, [[Thanium star-glaive]]s, and [[Cronian battlebird/Legends|Cronian battlebirds]] in mixed squadrons. Larger vessels, around the size of a [[Cruiser/Legends|cruiser]], included [[Cronese harpice]]s, [[Brigian penteconter]]s, [[Thanium polyreme]]s, [[Yutuski rakehell]]s and [[Xolochi Dreadnought]]s. Xim's [[Flagship/Legends|flagships]] included the ''[[Eibon Scimitar]]'' and the ''[[Deathknell]]''.

Xim's naval weapons were primarily [[Torpedo/Legends|torpedos]], [[Concussion missile/Legends|concussion missiles]], and [[Pulse cannon/Legends|pulse cannons]], which melted enemy hulls and disrupted circuitry with blasts of radiation. The Despot's ships were armored with [[Kiirium/Legends|kiirium]], a superconducting alloy that gave each ship a mirrored hull that could reflect enemy energy weapons. [[Mytag crystal/Legends|Mytag crystals]] were used in communications and sensor systems to protect them from electromagnetic attack. [[Bordhell-type fuel slug]]s gave Xim's warships unparalleled range and speed.
